fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity washington, how to transfer audiologist license to washington, audiologist compact washington, washington audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Washington Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.mailing_address:
Washington State Department of Health
PO Box 47877
Olympia, WA 98504-7877

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the American Academy of Audiology or its equivalent

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Washington.

steps.title: Create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) Account
steps.description: Navigate to the SecureAccess Washington (SAW) portal at secureaccess.wa.gov and create a new account, which is required to access state online services.

steps.title: Add the Credentialing System Service
steps.description: Log into your newly created SAW account. Select 'Add a Service' and then search for and add the 'WA Health Professions Licensing & Credentialing System' to your list of services.

steps.title: Complete the Online Audiologist Application
steps.description: Access the WA Health Professions Licensing & Credentialing System through your SAW account. Complete the online application for Audiologist licensure, providing all required personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload or mail all necessary supporting documents, which typically include official transcripts showing your Au.D. or equivalent degree, official Praxis exam scores, and verification of supervised practice experience.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $100 through the WA Health Professions Licensing & Credentialing System. This fee applies to both initial and endorsement applications.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application within the WA Health Professions Licensing & Credentialing System. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Department of Health to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Washington participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Washington without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Washington
hero.dek: Audiologists in Washington State must apply for licensure through the Washington State Department of Health's online 'WA Health Professions Licensing & Credentialing System', accessed via SecureAccess Washington (SAW).

official_links.registration_steps: First, create an account on SecureAccess Washington (SAW). Then, log into SAW and add the 'WA Health Professions Licensing & Credentialing System' to your services to access the application portal.
